project.title: Chunxi Road Spatiotemporal Intelligence Cockpit
project.subtitle: A lightweight frontend for the multimodal data foundation described in the proposal, now extended with a forecast lab for Transformer, Mamba, and hybrid next-hour predictions.

metrics.label: Weekly study-area signals
metrics.detail: Hourly mobile-signal observations aggregated across the master grid.

metrics.label: Study-area users
metrics.detail: Distinct anonymized user proxies within the valid Chunxi Road window.

metrics.label: Best forecast model
metrics.detail: Test Total MAE 27,003 over the held-out 22-hour window.

metrics.label: Hybrid forecast error
metrics.detail: Hybrid Transformer+Mamba total error across the held-out forecast window.

metrics.label: Peak fused video minute
metrics.detail: 17,196 fused detections across both camera views.

metrics.label: Static walkability context
metrics.detail: Pedestrian ways from the OSM mobility and subway package.

metrics.label: Curated perception records
metrics.detail: Public narrative samples about shopping, culture, and crowd experience.

pillars.title: Multimodal Resource Pool
pillars.subtitle: Data foundation from the proposal
pillars.description: The proposal calls for a unified urban data pool that links road networks, mobile signals, observation videos, street scenes, and public perception into one structured spatial system.

pillars.title: Spatiotemporal Pattern Engine
pillars.subtitle: Mamba plus Transformer logic
pillars.description: The dashboard foregrounds continuous sequence signals for Mamba-style modeling and cross-modal spatial context for Transformer-style association learning across activity, movement, and function.

pillars.title: AI Decision Service
pillars.subtitle: From evidence to recommendations
pillars.description: The final layer converts observed Chunxi Road patterns into stakeholder-facing guidance for residents, businesses, community operators, and planners.

datasets.name: Mobile Signal Master Grid
datasets.description: Hourly grid-level signal counts, inflow, outflow, stay duration, and leisure proxies provide the main activity surface for Chunxi Road.

datasets.name: Dual-camera Fused Observation
datasets.description: KFC and RF are fused into a single ALL stream for temporal pattern reading and spatial heatmap display, avoiding misleading camera-name interpretation.

datasets.name: Multimodal Forecast Outputs
datasets.description: The new forecast package compares the hybrid Transformer+Mamba model with Transformer-only and Mamba-only branches, exposing test accuracy, gate weights, and core grid forecasts.

datasets.name: OSM Mobility and Subway Context
datasets.description: The OSM package contributes walkability, crossings, and subway access features that can be engineered into the master grid.

datasets.name: Public Perception Texts
datasets.description: Curated social and platform content gives qualitative context about crowding, culture, shopping, and street image, but it still needs better spatiotemporal alignment.

datasets.name: Street-view Pilot Scores
datasets.description: A small batch of street-scene scores gives an initial read on greenery, built form, traffic presence, and beauty perception.

video.labelNote: KFC and RF only distinguish the two camera angles. The fused ALL stream is the main evidence layer for temporal and spatial movement patterns.

streetScene.note: Current street-scene coverage is a pilot sample rather than a full street-view survey.

summaryCards.label: Hybrid test error
summaryCards.detail: Total next-hour absolute error across the 22 test windows.

summaryCards.label: Signal gate weight
summaryCards.detail: Average hybrid attention weight assigned to the dynamic mobility stream.

summaryCards.label: External coverage
summaryCards.detail: Video cameras and aligned social days currently feeding the multimodal bundle.

highlights.title: Saturday evening is the real pressure point
highlights.detail: The test-period actual peak lands at 2025-03-22 18:00 with 354,429 total signals.

highlights.title: The hybrid branch still predicts the peak too early
highlights.detail: The hybrid model peaks at 2025-03-22 12:00 with 340,312 predicted signals, showing a midday-leading bias under the current external modalities.

highlights.title: Dynamic mobility is still the dominant modality
highlights.detail: Hybrid test gates average dynamic 80.0%, video 12.4%, social 1.3%, and static 6.3%.

highlights.title: Core forecast difficulty stays in the retail spine
highlights.detail: The largest hybrid total-error hour is 2025-03-22 09:00 and the highest-error cells remain concentrated around grids 3_1, 3_0, and 4_1.

patterns.title: Evening retail pulse dominates the weekly rhythm
patterns.evidence: The highest observed signal hour is 2025-03-22 18:00 with 354,429 signals.
patterns.implication: Late afternoon and early evening are the most sensitive windows for congestion, crowd management, and retail activation in the Chunxi Road core.

patterns.title: Dual-camera fusion reveals the key pedestrian pulse
patterns.evidence: The fused ALL stream peaks at 2026-03-29 15:12 with 17,196 detections across both camera views.
patterns.implication: Operational monitoring and public-facing storytelling should prioritise the fused time pattern rather than the individual KFC and RF labels.

patterns.title: Activity concentrates in the Taikoo Li and Chunxi corridor
patterns.evidence: Grid 3_1 records 2,540,720 weekly signals and is dominated by midday activity.
patterns.implication: The central retail spine should be treated as the anchor zone for footfall forecasting, public-space design, and targeted commercial programming.

patterns.title: The strongest OD exchange is a short central loop
patterns.evidence: The top corridor runs from 3_1 to 4_1 with 196,306 weekly movements.
patterns.implication: Micro-mobility management, storefront sequencing, and wayfinding interventions should prioritize short-range transfers between adjacent retail cells.

patterns.title: Road and subway infrastructure frame the pedestrian core
patterns.evidence: The OSM package includes 154 pedestrian ways, 20 crossings, and 5 subway entrances.
patterns.implication: Static accessibility features can be joined to the signal grid to explain why some cells outperform neighboring ones at similar times of day.

patterns.title: Online perception is more observational than polarised
patterns.evidence: The largest social-perception bucket is 正向 with 31 documents in the current curated sample.
patterns.implication: Narrative content is useful for texture and qualitative interpretation, but it should complement rather than replace behavioral signals.

chatPrompts: When is Chunxi Road most crowded, and what should businesses do about it?
Which OD corridors deserve the most wayfinding attention?
What does the fused dual-camera heatmap reveal about Chunxi Road movement?
What should planners prioritise if they want to improve walkability near the subway entrances?
How can we combine the signal grid and the video trajectories for Transformer and Mamba modeling?
Which forecast model performs best on the held-out Chunxi Road window?
What do the hybrid gate weights imply about the current multimodal data quality?
